target.title: fix(agents): count streamed model deltas incrementally
target.body:
## Summary
- Count streamed `text_delta`/`thinking_delta`/`toolcall_delta` diagnostics from their incremental `delta` bytes instead of serializing the full event.
- Drop growing `partial` snapshots from fallback stream byte accounting so diagnostics do not repeatedly walk the accumulated assistant message.
- Add regression coverage proving text-delta accounting does not serialize large partial snapshots.

Refs #86599.

## Verification
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- AWS Crabbox `check:changed`: provider `aws`, lease `cbx_2d9c07ee303f`, run `run_9353442837ce`, exit 0

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: dense model streams no longer make model-call diagnostics serialize the full growing `partial` assistant message on every delta.
Real environment tested: AWS Crabbox Linux changed gate plus focused local Vitest wrapper in a Codex worktree.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --class standard --idle-timeout 30m --stop-after success --shell -- 'set -euo pipefail; corepack pnpm check:changed'`
Evidence after fix: `run_9353442837ce` completed with exit 0 on provider `aws`, lease `cbx_2d9c07ee303f`.
Observed result after fix: core/coreTests changed lanes passed, including typecheck, lint, guards, and import-cycle checks.
What was not tested: a live Windows local-model starvation repro; this PR removes the diagnostic quadratic work but does not claim to close the whole issue.
